title: Charred Shattering Croatian Mincemeat Tagine
description: A hearty, paleo-friendly solo dinner featuring charred mincemeat and a hint of cure. Perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Preheat a ceramic tagine over medium heat.
Sear the ground pork in the rendered lamb fat until browned, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add a pinch of cure to the mincemeat and stir to combine.
Chop 2 cloves of garlic and add them to the tagine, stirring to prevent burning.
Add 250ml of stock to the tagine and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer, covered, for 30 minutes.
While the tagine simmers, prepare a bed of mixed greens.
To assemble the dish, place a portion of the mixed greens in the bottom of a bowl and top with a portion of the charred mincemeat.
Garnish with a sprig of parsley and serve immediately.
Reheat the tagine over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the mincemeat is heated through.
Serve hot, garnished with a sprinkle of paprika.
Chop a handful of parsley and stir it into the remaining mincemeat.
Spoon the parsley and mincemeat mixture into the bottom of the tagine and smooth the top.
Brush the lid of the tagine with a little lamb fat and replace the lid.
Place the tagine over medium heat and simmer, covered, for 10 minutes, or until the meat is heated through.
